---
title: "Personal assistant profile"
description: "Bedrock inference only. No AWS API surface, no security services."
---

<Info>
Personal assistant gives the agent **only Bedrock `InvokeModel`** permissions. Zero other AWS access. Use it when you want AI help but don't want the agent anywhere near your infrastructure.
</Info>

## What it is

The minimal-surface profile. The instance has:

- Outbound Bedrock `InvokeModel` / `InvokeModelWithResponseStream` only
- No IAM except the bedrock-scoped policy
- No security services (they're off by default to keep cost minimal)

Best for:

- Running a coding agent against Bedrock without broader AWS exposure
- NemoClaw (mandatory — NemoClaw's OpenShell sandbox blocks AWS APIs anyway)
- Personal productivity agents where the only question is "can I invoke the model"

## Defaults (simple mode)

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| IAM policy | Bedrock invoke (scoped to specific model ARNs) |
| Instance type | `t4g.medium` (2 vCPU, 4 GiB, arm64) |
| Root volume | 40 GB |
| Data volume | 80 GB (OpenClaw / NemoClaw) / 0 GB (others) |
| Security Hub | ❌ **off** |
| GuardDuty | ❌ **off** |
| Inspector | ❌ **off** |
| Access Analyzer | ❌ **off** |
| Config recorder | ❌ **off** |

The security services are off because they cost money and there's nothing in the account surface to monitor — the agent has no write ability.

## Install examples

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="NemoClaw (required profile)">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack nemoclaw \
      --profile personal_assistant
    ```
    NemoClaw **only** works with personal_assistant. It runs in an OpenShell sandbox.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="OpenClaw personal">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack openclaw \
      --profile personal_assistant
    ```
    OpenClaw gateway for coding help; can't touch AWS.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Claude Code personal">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack claude-code \
      --profile personal_assistant
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## When to use personal assistant

- You want the agent to help you code, but not *touch* your AWS.
- You're running NemoClaw (it's the only valid profile).
- You want to minimize cost — security services off means just the EC2 + Bedrock calls.

## When to NOT use personal assistant

- The agent should deploy code or provision AWS resources → use [builder](/profiles/builder).
- The agent should explain/audit existing AWS → use [account_assistant](/profiles/account-assistant).

## Enabling security services anyway

Advanced mode lets you enable them individually even on personal_assistant if you want the monitoring. See [Security services](/reference/security-services).
